codeReview.summary: The fix commit cleanly addresses the prior blocking issues: it removes the runtime `eprintln!` side effect from `resolve_store_ref_record_id` and keeps the follow-up diff scoped to runtime/test files only. The updated integration coverage now explicitly exercises a create-if-missing nested-reference path while preserving the existing FK-only raw-parent assertions and hydrated caller-facing values.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the updated rust-library-worker guidance better than the original failed feature: the fix commit stayed isolated to runtime/test files, suggesting the new commit-scope check added to the skill resolved the earlier scope-mixing failure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Prior failed commit 08a62275 touched `macros/src/lib.rs`, `macros/Cargo.toml`, and trybuild snapshot files, while fix commit 2a079c2 only changes `core/src/lib.rs` and `core/tests/integration_db.rs`; `rust-library-worker/SKILL.md` now includes step 11 requiring final diff inspection for feature scope.

summary: Review passed: the fix removes the normal-path stderr side effect, keeps the change within runtime scope, and adds focused nested single/optional coverage without introducing new blocking issues.
